Tommy Tuberville isn’t just a senator — he’s a fighter. Since arriving in Washington, he has been one of the most determined conservatives standing up to Joe Biden’s disastrous policies. **Now, as he prepares for re-election, it’s up to us to make sure he has the support he needs to keep battling for America.**

Before politics, Coach Tuberville built championship teams on the football field. He knows what it takes to win, and he’s brought that same leadership to the Senate. When it comes to standing up for our conservative values, he never backs down from a fight.
---
[ ](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=aa37be3e208047599464de64f2742956&_e=g1znFvf0itH7WYtkyg8rHIvGEA1J2IZhB_h_N7-O2QkUd1KIfQMEQx-xnIGmTu3s-i0gY9Lf5ntT7JJpetNF3BMjpyK35pui0xcYkC_RSzzJbRLGLyAaeE5gFhiGgvV-1v-3UrxOiK89jABdPECghmh5-OBZehklRU_uLBLQXdGt42iiIJ91WjqzXAbxT_u1aFcXchB-IAPMeX8K3zo2BWh1DzdOF6p7g2oMHwBmc8I8L4CqIYIP_KHT9ErU-kBY)
---
He proved that when he took on the Pentagon’s outrageous policy of funding abortion travel with taxpayer dollars. While others stayed silent, Coach Tuberville stood firm. His pressure ultimately forced the Pentagon to reverse course — a major victory for life and for American taxpayers. Now Coach Tuberville is leading the charge to protect women’s sports from being undermined by radical gender ideology. His bill to enshrine President Trump's executive action into law is common sense, but Democrats refuse to accept reality. Thankfully, Coach isn’t afraid to call them out and fight for policies that promote truth and fairness. **Sadly, the Left is already gearing up to take him down. They know how effective he is, and they’ll do everything they can to defeat him in 2026. We can’t let that happen.** **[**Will you step up with a contribution to help Coach Tuberville?**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=aa37be3e208047599464de64f2742956&_e=g1znFvf0itH7WYtkyg8rHIvGEA1J2IZhB_h_N7-O2QkUd1KIfQMEQx-xnIGmTu3s-i0gY9Lf5ntT7JJpetNF3BMjpyK35pui0xcYkC_RSzymL0lZRGinQMWz4JBs89GBKDPGddsew6cMb1QF-p-p6mQ2EftDiCEOBq0etD9LjshearnCmSNhe2cH7LiQugW0ia6qq_18v4d8VdBQ-sa0hvTPfKRjKJ0IdriOtRY8eYjhE18cPjB6QVe8L91o75T-)** _The Senate Conservatives Fund will pay all processing fees and transfer 100% of your contribution directly to his campaign. It's the best way to support him because we don't use gimmicks that secretly redirect huge chunks of your contribution to other committees and digital vendors._ Coach Tuberville has never wavered in his commitment to our values. _**Now, it’s time for us to have his back.**_ [**Support Coach Tuberville today!**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=aa37be3e208047599464de64f2742956&_e=g1znFvf0itH7WYtkyg8rHIvGEA1J2IZhB_h_N7-O2QkUd1KIfQMEQx-xnIGmTu3s-i0gY9Lf5ntT7JJpetNF3BMjpyK35pui0xcYkC_RSzxg3jIb6t5mVBZ0v_jyg0eFZueHBS7WnQ3jSLS37T4PFXvX8L0q9MsQbxIFomasHUSbg_aKJEf6L2WCCQLAn3m9_Uc64BMOplw70jl3OjSun_VlITf8fg1zyu7Mu4QYi0dlYJVQqBYlqSG-WNZ7U-2p) Thank you for considering this request and for doing so much to help fight for America's future.

clean_body: | | | | Tommy Tuberville isn’t just a senator — he’s a fighter. Since arriving in Washington, he has been one of the most determined conservatives standing up to Joe Biden’s disastrous policies. Now, as he prepares for re-election, it’s up to us to make sure he has the support he needs to keep battling for America. Before politics, Coach Tuberville built championship teams on the football field. He knows what it takes to win, and he’s brought that same leadership to the Senate. When it comes to standing up for our conservative values, he never backs down from a fight. He proved that when he took on the Pentagon’s outrageous policy of funding abortion travel with taxpayer dollars. While others stayed silent, Coach Tuberville stood firm. His pressure ultimately forced the Pentagon to reverse course — a major victory for life and for American taxpayers. Now Coach Tuberville is leading the charge to protect women’s sports from being undermined by radical gender ideology. His bill to enshrine President Trump's executive action into law is common sense, but Democrats refuse to accept reality. Thankfully, Coach isn’t afraid to call them out and fight for policies that promote truth and fairness. Sadly, the Left is already gearing up to take him down. They know how effective he is, and they’ll do everything they can to defeat him in 2026. We can’t let that happen. - The Senate Conservatives Fund will pay all processing fees and transfer 100% of your contribution directly to his campaign. It's the best way to support him because we don't use gimmicks that secretly redirect huge chunks of your contribution to other committees and digital vendors. Coach Tuberville has never wavered in his commitment to our values. Now, it’s time for us to have his back. Thank you for considering this request and for doing so much to help fight for America's future. | END OF MONTH COUNTDOWN | Secure America's future. Support Senate Conservatives. Hey team,

Now that we’re a few weeks into the new Trump administration and, if you’re like us, there are a lot of different things that are causing a wide range of emotions.

In the new age of Trump, we’re working to build up our local Democratic infrastructure, recruit new candidates to run for office, and resist Trump extremism in any way we can. Of course, the 2024 election showed us that we need to be even more responsive to the needs of our voters.

[**So, can you fill out our quick Winter Survey so we can hear directly from you about what matters and what you’re thinking?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101791063/527074424/-1578996237?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDU1NzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2ZhMDBhM2YtNDBlNi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=PtRoLjfIiGaTuXqtIVcENh4FeLFfbIeEW7VSqGvS5EY=&emci=6aac902c-94e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=cfa00a3f-40e6-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=81601)

The Power of Belonging

# [America's Masculinity Crisis Ch. 2](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=156742962&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NzQyOTYyLCJpYXQiOjE3MzkwMzUwNjEsImV4cCI6MTc0MTYyNzA2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.InFInQJ2eussbC7-2NU_PZco4jc34TsoDjHMJdI6S5s)
### The Power of Belonging
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Feb 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=156742962&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NzQyOTYyLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczOTAzNTA2MSwiZXhwIjoxNzQxNjI3MDYxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0._SqvI1b4BpHkXOWTA0zuCYKVFZy5LFhAjerzXOZzrVA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=156742962&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NzQyOTYyLCJpYXQiOjE3MzkwMzUwNjEsImV4cCI6MTc0MTYyNzA2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.InFInQJ2eussbC7-2NU_PZco4jc34TsoDjHMJdI6S5s&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=156742962&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NzQyOTYyLCJpYXQiOjE3MzkwMzUwNjEsImV4cCI6MTc0MTYyNzA2MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.InFInQJ2eussbC7-2NU_PZco4jc34TsoDjHMJdI6S5s)
---
| | [](https://substack..l3o_3G8WK1HOlDaHGocyigdf4RdfjjqxCv-imqZ44ho?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/americas-masculinity-crisis-ch-2?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

In [my last post](https://substack.com/redirect/72d2751c-1afd-4a51-9baf-83a7f509e926?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I outlined some similarities between the present day and the McCarthyism crisis of the 1950's, when President Kennedy published his famous book _Profiles in Courage._
In response to our current situation, and the obsession of some with America's so-called "masculinity crisis," I began a series of profiles amplifying just a few of the thousands of acts of common strength and resolve that play out across the fabric of Missouri and our country every day-- quietly holding us together while our leaders seek to rip us apart. Examples that could dig us out of this crisis if only they got the attention or recognition that has instead been lavished on the dividers.
I [began with my best friend's dad](https://substack.com/redirect/72d2751c-1afd-4a51-9baf-83a7f509e926?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a war refugee, and the strength he showed by moving forward rather than looking back. Today, the man who taught me what it means to belong to something, and how we could all use more of that in our lives.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.A2EATGJIjiadF5u6wY4Wkc_s7EnTaAKoULsYaN2tiCI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=156742962)
* * *
As many of you know, I was activated by the Marine Corps to serve at the Pentagon for the next few months. As part of those duties, I got to come home this week to Mizzou's Law School to tell the students about life as a Marine Officer and attorney and see if any of them wanted to sign up.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7ee91ff7-0141-477c-9229-10acae5564ab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_With fellow veteran and Chiefs fan Dean Bob Bailey of Mizzou Law School (The views expressed in this Substack are those of the individual only and not those of the Department of Defense or the University of Missouri. Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.)_
I was impressed at how much interest there was from the attendees and folks who just walked by. It was incredible to see how many from our younger generations want to serve or give back to the community.
It was also disappointing to discover that almost none of them think public service is financially viable for them. Many felt forced to go in one direction when they would rather be doing something that they feel is more meaningful.
Fortunately, I was able to let them in on what must be the world's biggest secret: that being a Marine Judge Advocate actually pays well!
The compensation package starts in the mid $100k's, varying on when you sign up and where you are stationed, and it includes 30 days of paid vacation per year, all federal holidays, immediate courtroom action trying your own cases, VA home loans, the GI Bill, free healthcare for you and the family, etc. Plus money for law school, bar exam prep, bar dues, etc.
Most importantly, it comes with a sense of service and belonging that is hard to come by these days.
Interest went through the roof once people realized it was a viable choice. And it was obvious from the engagement that most of these kids wanted to get out and see the country and world, mix with others from all over, and do something meaningful.
As much as I love the military and what it has made possible for me, which I wrote about in _[Running for Office as a Normal Person](https://substack.com/redirect/42127344-c25b-42a0-ad74-737336a6d844?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , I think me should have a multitude of options for public service with decent pay and benefits.

_(As an aside, if you or someone you know might be interested in becoming a Marine Judge Advocate, whether they are a law student or already a lawyer, let me know in the comments and I will message you for details. I 'm happy to talk to anyone about it.)_
I suspect one of the things causing our country so much pain right now is that it's much harder to feel like you belong to something real anymore. Social media is so isolating, and pushes all the wrong things. Corporate America has sacrificed everything to maximizing shareholder profits. Small businesses have been gutted by consolidation. Communities are divided.
I was lucky in my life to get that sense of belonging and purpose as a United States Marine, but even luckier to have discovered its importance in High School, so that I could look for it later in life.
Here is the story of how that happened, and the man who made it so.
* * *
"I'll be alright," I said, flinching as I put a bag of ice on my shin and leaned back against a Jeff City High School locker.
Coach looked down at me and pursed his lips.
"I'll be right back," he said
Two minutes later he returned and held something down to me.
I looked at his hand and back to his face, confused. He was handing me one of those paper concession stand coca cola cups.
"Thanks, Coach..." I said, confused, "but I'm good."
I nodded toward my water bottle.
He lifted the cup back up to himself, tilting it sideways as he did so. Sitting right below him, I jerked instinctively, expecting cold water to pour down on me. But nothing happened.
Bemused, I watched as he ripped the paper rim right off the cup about three inches down. Then I laughed at myself. The water was frozen. With the rim torn off, it looked like a giant push pop sticking out of the tattered end of the cup.
"This won't fix things, but it's better than the bag," Coach said, "Do it like this." And he rubbed the icy end up and down his arm then handed it back down to me.
"We'll figure this out," he added, pointing at my shin, and walked away.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4fa8bb8d-ac0c-4007-9054-2c192a5b3f11?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Taking off, Waynesville, Missouri_
It was the fall of 1998 and we were a third of the way through my junior season of high school cross country. For the first time in my running life, I was injured. But I knew that Coach, who had been there from my first high school run, really would do everything he could to figure it out.
Just like my academic career, my athletic performance at Lewis and Clark Middle School had been entirely unremarkable. I'm pretty sure the middle school coaches only knew who I was because I had gotten my ear-pierced in seventh grade and my mom gave them hell when they made me take it out and it grew over.
I had decided to run cross country freshman year of high school on a whim, basically because my super cool older cousin ran cross country and I didn't really have anything else to do that fall.
_**What a decision.**_
On my first run, I paired up with a guy named Randy and we were given directions on where to run. It could not have been longer than three miles, but we stopped and walked every few of minutes, thinking we were dying. We would have walked a lot more except that the girls team started on the same route a few minutes after we did and, after their leaders tormented us as they flew by, we didn't want to be caught walking by the rest of them as they all caught up and passed us.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a0a7d87e-68f4-4343-99aa-497963d3cea2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Freshman Year_
Miraculously, by the first meet Coach had us freshman to a point where we could at least run a full two mile freshman course without stopping. And midway through the season he had us on the occasional slow run with older guys, who we both worshiped and were terrified of.
They were crazy fast -- under Coach's leadership we had become one of the top teams in the largest division in the state. But they were also just plain crazy. They used to pick up discarded beer cans on long country runs and drink whatever was left in them. They chewed dip when no one was looking and gave us nicknames like "Buddy," "Daisy," and "Ep." They tried to name me "Dammit," like, "dammit run faster" or "dammit get over here," but Coach didn't let that or several more offensive labels related to the pronunciation of my last name stick.
By the end of freshman year Coach had me running 5k in under 18 minutes, which wasn't half bad. I might have been a varsity runner if we hadn't been state champions that year.
I tore through sophomore season and aspired to be an all-state runner like so many of the older guys. Despite leading a team that was ranked as high as 13th in the nation that year, Coach took the time to make a plan for me so that I could make it happen the next year. The summer before junior year I crushed the miles like never before and just knew we were going to make it happen.
The first couple of meets made me even more certain. But then, as the mileage piled up, my shins started to break down. I'd never experienced shin splints before, so I ignored it at first, as something I could just run through.
This, of course, just made it worse. One day, Coach spotted me limping during a workout and immediately stopped me. I took the next day off, but it didn't make a difference.
Shortly after he gave me the ice cup, Coach came up with a new plan to at least keep me in the mix. He got me access to the YMCA across the street from the high school. Until things got better, I ran in the deep end of the pool with a floaty belt every morning before school and was either back in the pool or on a bike each afternoon.
I spent the rest of the season in and out of the pool. A doctor told me that my feet were different sizes, so maybe I could try getting the right size for each rather than splitting the difference. This came across as pretty ridiculous given that my parents could only afford one pair of shoes for the entire summer and fall as it was and everything I earned went toward gas, lunch, and saving for a beat up old car so that I could get to work and school on my own. I told Coach about the situation and we tried some inserts instead. They didn't do much.
Coach's pool regime worked fairly well. I ran the post-season that year and helped our team medal at the state meet. And although I didn't make all-state, I had a good showing for a guy who hadn't run in over a month, and scored in a year where we won a 4th place State trophy.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7f33b479-8c1d-4def-8818-303426e8f7fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Jeff City Jays 1998 Cross Country, 4th Place State_
As senior year came around and I amped up my mileage that summer, I freaked out at every twinge in my shin. I told Coach about it and he told me to ice early and often and not to worry about it. He said he would take care of things, and he gave me the occasional morning off so I wouldn't get too worn down. I began the year even better than the year before, but the nagging thought that the mileage would catch up was always there.
I brought it up again and Coach looked at me firmly and said, "Lucas, don't worry about it. I've got it figured out."
A couple weeks later he asked me to stay after practice. So I did. He avoided eye contact with me as everyone else headed out. I started to develop that knot in my stomach that you get when you know you're in trouble for something. I wondered what it could be.
He waited until everyone was gone. Then he called me over to the storage closet in the hallway where he could talk without anyone overhearing us. My stomach churned. What had I done this time?
Coach turned toward me as I walked through the door. I guess I was going to find out the hard way. I stood there awkwardly and waited for him to talk. I had learned that strategy the hard way when dealing with my mother. Always better for the adult to go first when you're in trouble.
"I want you to have these," he said quietly, "but you can't tell anyone. Do you understand?"
"Huh?"
"I've been tracking your miles. You need to change your old ones out. But I know... well... everyone's situation is different. Just take these and don't tell anyone."
He shoved a cardboard box into my hands and walked out, not waiting for me to say thank you or see what it was. As he walked away I looked down.
It was a new pair of running shoes.

Later that fall, injury free, I proudly posed for the Jefferson City News Tribune photographer on the grounds of Oak Hills Golf Course for a photo after the state track meet, wearing my hard-earned all-state medal. Captain of the Jefferson City High School Cross Country Team.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e71ba0d3-1087-4c79-b1de-65455a97f203?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_All-State 4A Cross Country, 1999, wearing Coach 's shoes_
I never would have achieved that without Coach. I may have never made it to Yale without the credentials he helped me earn. Or had the confidence that comes from knowing people have your back.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e249d02e-63d6-47e0-b5fd-d1b3ce50b8a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
That simple act, which I'm sure was against some rule somewhere that makes life harder for people without money, likely changed the course of my life. And it's not like Coach did that for me from a place of comfort. He was a middle school teacher and high school coach in a state that pays its teachers poorly.
And yet, he did big and little things like that for all of us. Most importantly, he gave us a sense of belonging and a place in the world. He taught us, from day one, to take care of ourselves and each other. That we always ran as a pack.
Our Cross Country team from that era is being inducted into the Missouri sports hall of fame this month. And we couldn't be prouder. We all still feel like we belong to it. And many of us, including me, have our place in this world because of Jim Marshall, one of the most selfless people I have ever known.
Lucas

(As an aside, if you or someone you know might be interested in becoming a Marine Judge Advocate, whether they are a law student or already a lawyer, let me know in the comments and I will message you for details. I 'm happy to talk to anyone about it.) I suspect one of the things causing our country so much pain right now is that it's much harder to feel like you belong to something real anymore. Social media is so isolating, and pushes all the wrong things. Corporate America has sacrificed everything to maximizing shareholder profits. Small businesses have been gutted by consolidation. Communities are divided. I was lucky in my life to get that sense of belonging and purpose as a United States Marine, but even luckier to have discovered its importance in High School, so that I could look for it later in life. Here is the story of how that happened, and the man who made it so. "I'll be alright," I said, flinching as I put a bag of ice on my shin and leaned back against a Jeff City High School locker. Coach looked down at me and pursed his lips. "I'll be right back," he said Two minutes later he returned and held something down to me. I looked at his hand and back to his face, confused. He was handing me one of those paper concession stand coca cola cups. "Thanks, Coach..." I said, confused, "but I'm good." I nodded toward my water bottle. He lifted the cup back up to himself, tilting it sideways as he did so. Sitting right below him, I jerked instinctively, expecting cold water to pour down on me. But nothing happened. Bemused, I watched as he ripped the paper rim right off the cup about three inches down. Then I laughed at myself. The water was frozen. With the rim torn off, it looked like a giant push pop sticking out of the tattered end of the cup. "This won't fix things, but it's better than the bag," Coach said, "Do it like this." And he rubbed the icy end up and down his arm then handed it back down to me. "We'll figure this out," he added, pointing at my shin, and walked away. | | ---|---|--- Taking off, Waynesville, Missouri It was the fall of 1998 and we were a third of the way through my junior season of high school cross country. For the first time in my running life, I was injured. But I knew that Coach, who had been there from my first high school run, really would do everything he could to figure it out. Just like my academic career, my athletic performance at Lewis and Clark Middle School had been entirely unremarkable. I'm pretty sure the middle school coaches only knew who I was because I had gotten my ear-pierced in seventh grade and my mom gave them hell when they made me take it out and it grew over. I had decided to run cross country freshman year of high school on a whim, basically because my super cool older cousin ran cross country and I didn't really have anything else to do that fall. What a decision. On my first run, I paired up with a guy named Randy and we were given directions on where to run. It could not have been longer than three miles, but we stopped and walked every few of minutes, thinking we were dying. We would have walked a lot more except that the girls team started on the same route a few minutes after we did and, after their leaders tormented us as they flew by, we didn't want to be caught walking by the rest of them as they all caught up and passed us. | | ---|---|--- Freshman Year Miraculously, by the first meet Coach had us freshman to a point where we could at least run a full two mile freshman course without stopping. And midway through the season he had us on the occasional slow run with older guys, who we both worshiped and were terrified of. They were crazy fast -- under Coach's leadership we had become one of the top teams in the largest division in the state. But they were also just plain crazy. They used to pick up discarded beer cans on long country runs and drink whatever was left in them. They chewed dip when no one was looking and gave us nicknames like "Buddy," "Daisy," and "Ep." They tried to name me "Dammit," like, "dammit run faster" or "dammit get over here," but Coach didn't let that or several more offensive labels related to the pronunciation of my last name stick. By the end of freshman year Coach had me running 5k in under 18 minutes, which wasn't half bad. I might have been a varsity runner if we hadn't been state champions that year. I tore through sophomore season and aspired to be an all-state runner like so many of the older guys. Despite leading a team that was ranked as high as 13th in the nation that year, Coach took the time to make a plan for me so that I could make it happen the next year. The summer before junior year I crushed the miles like never before and just knew we were going to make it happen. The first couple of meets made me even more certain. But then, as the mileage piled up, my shins started to break down. I'd never experienced shin splints before, so I ignored it at first, as something I could just run through. This, of course, just made it worse. One day, Coach spotted me limping during a workout and immediately stopped me. I took the next day off, but it didn't make a difference. Shortly after he gave me the ice cup, Coach came up with a new plan to at least keep me in the mix. He got me access to the YMCA across the street from the high school. Until things got better, I ran in the deep end of the pool with a floaty belt every morning before school and was either back in the pool or on a bike each afternoon. I spent the rest of the season in and out of the pool. A doctor told me that my feet were different sizes, so maybe I could try getting the right size for each rather than splitting the difference. This came across as pretty ridiculous given that my parents could only afford one pair of shoes for the entire summer and fall as it was and everything I earned went toward gas, lunch, and saving for a beat up old car so that I could get to work and school on my own. I told Coach about the situation and we tried some inserts instead. They didn't do much. Coach's pool regime worked fairly well. I ran the post-season that year and helped our team medal at the state meet. And although I didn't make all-state, I had a good showing for a guy who hadn't run in over a month, and scored in a year where we won a 4th place State trophy. | | ---|---|--- Jeff City Jays 1998 Cross Country, 4th Place State As senior year came around and I amped up my mileage that summer, I freaked out at every twinge in my shin. I told Coach about it and he told me to ice early and often and not to worry about it. He said he would take care of things, and he gave me the occasional morning off so I wouldn't get too worn down. I began the year even better than the year before, but the nagging thought that the mileage would catch up was always there. I brought it up again and Coach looked at me firmly and said, "Lucas, don't worry about it. I've got it figured out." A couple weeks later he asked me to stay after practice. So I did. He avoided eye contact with me as everyone else headed out. I started to develop that knot in my stomach that you get when you know you're in trouble for something. I wondered what it could be. He waited until everyone was gone. Then he called me over to the storage closet in the hallway where he could talk without anyone overhearing us. My stomach churned. What had I done this time? Coach turned toward me as I walked through the door. I guess I was going to find out the hard way. I stood there awkwardly and waited for him to talk. I had learned that strategy the hard way when dealing with my mother. Always better for the adult to go first when you're in trouble. "I want you to have these," he said quietly, "but you can't tell anyone. Do you understand?" "Huh?" "I've been tracking your miles. You need to change your old ones out. But I know... well... everyone's situation is different. Just take these and don't tell anyone." He shoved a cardboard box into my hands and walked out, not waiting for me to say thank you or see what it was. As he walked away I looked down. It was a new pair of running shoes. Later that fall, injury free, I proudly posed for the Jefferson City News Tribune photographer on the grounds of Oak Hills Golf Course for a photo after the state track meet, wearing my hard-earned all-state medal. Captain of the Jefferson City High School Cross Country Team. | | ---|---|--- All-State 4A Cross Country, 1999, wearing Coach 's shoes I never would have achieved that without Coach. I may have never made it to Yale without the credentials he helped me earn. Or had the confidence that comes from knowing people have your back. | | ---|---|--- That simple act, which I'm sure was against some rule somewhere that makes life harder for people without money, likely changed the course of my life. And it's not like Coach did that for me from a place of comfort. He was a middle school teacher and high school coach in a state that pays its teachers poorly. And yet, he did big and little things like that for all of us. Most importantly, he gave us a sense of belonging and a place in the world. He taught us, from day one, to take care of ourselves and each other. That we always ran as a pack. Our Cross Country team from that era is being inducted into the Missouri sports hall of fame this month. And we couldn't be prouder. We all still feel like we belong to it. And many of us, including me, have our place in this world because of Jim Marshall, one of the most selfless people I have ever known. Lucas Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Lucas's Substack, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Lucas Kunce P.O. Box 1240, Independence, MO 64050 ? ?

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: Trump’s unqualified and dangerous cabinet picks will be a disaster for our planet
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
Scientists aren’t just sounding the alarm – they’re banging down the door as the Earth’s temperature continues to rise due to the endless burning of fossil fuels.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101791189/527074844/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMWZlNjE2MC00MGU2LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Pf3h3wcRNI5Cr3MpcOXyPjQ2AS4-hbMlMyfU3p1IaBc=&emci=02cf8a6c-a6e5-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=a1fe6160-40e6-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=7731215)
---

| | **Scientists aren't just sounding the alarm – they're banging down the door as the Earth's temperature continues to rise due to the endless burning of fossil fuels.** 2024 was the hottest year on record, and across the country, Americans are struggling with the impacts of climate chaos on a daily basis. Devastating wildfires are blanketing our communities with air pollution, extreme drought threatens our food supply, and intensifying storms leave behind record-level flooding and damage. **_The bottom line is we can't keep living like this._** For our sake and for the sake of future generations, we need leaders with the courage to stand up to Big Oil and fight for our future. Instead, Trump's fossil fuel cronies are turning our government into an oil executive's dream team that will put corporate greed over our health, our planet, and our children's future. That's why I opposed 3 key Trump Cabinet picks: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101791190/527074845/-2122444374?refcode=2025-02-08-enviro&recurring=&amount=12&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=a1fe6160-40e6-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMWZlNjE2MC00MGU2LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Pf3h3wcRNI5Cr3MpcOXyPjQ2AS4-hbMlMyfU3p1IaBc=) The MAGA-led Senate rubber-stamped all three of these extremist Cabinet secretaries, but I will do everything I can to stop them from turning our public lands into corporate playgrounds and our air and water into dumping grounds. The stakes couldn't be higher, and we need climate champions at every level who will push back and build a cleaner, greener future. name: Sara Jacobs
subject: Democrats’ 4-part strategy to counter Elon Musk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
Friends, there is a lot going on right now.
I know things can feel overwhelming or confusing or both – so let’s break it down.
First, some basics on the Constitution:
* The Constitution gives *Congress* the power of the purse – meaning it is Congress’s responsibility to decide how money gets spent.
* Congress is also who makes the laws.
* The role of the executive branch is to implement the laws that Congress makes.
So what is happening right now?
First, Donald Trump tried to freeze all federal funding (meaning all Congressionally appropriated spending that Congress has already voted on how to spend).
Second, Elon Musk has tried to dismantle USAID, the agency that was Congressionally created and primarily responsible for U.S. foreign assistance.
And third, Elon Musk and his private team (which is apparently made up of six young men aged 19-24 who have never worked in government before) are trying to gain access to really sensitive and critical payment systems and people’s personal information.
All together, these actions amount to an attempted coup.
This is illegal. The executive branch has to follow the laws that Congress has passed – they can’t just do whatever they want. That’s not how our government works!
So, obviously... this is bad. It’s very, very bad and very dangerous. And while Democrats don’t have as many tools available to us in the minority, we are taking action.
Here’s our four-part strategy to counter this attempted coup:
1. We’re introducing legislation to address some of these things , like stopping Elon Musk’s takeover of USAID or his team’s access to sensitive systems. This is the main action Members of Congress have the power to take, and we are absolutely doing it.

2. Lawsuits are already underway , and we’ve seen that they can be successful. Federal judges have already forced a pause on the federal funding freeze Trump tried to put in place, and blocked his executive order trying to end birthright citizenship. It’s important to note that Congress cannot itself file lawsuits – so we need to work with outside partners, like Democratic state attorneys general or impacted nonprofit organizations – to be able to use this tool.

3. Congress will do our constitutionally mandated oversight. I went with my colleagues this week to USAID HQ and the Treasury Department to see what’s happening with our own eyes, and we will keep demanding answers.

4. Lastly, we need you. Public pressure is the best tool that we have to actually push back on what Donald Trump is doing and get him to pause or reverse it. Speak out about how his actions are affecting you. Organize within your community. Bring this up with your friends.
I know things feel chaotic, especially with our 24-hour news cycle with frenzied coverage of every comment Trump makes. But please don’t tune out – we need you and your voice now more than ever before.
Thanks for reading,
Sara

California’s 51st District

clean_body: [] Friends, there is a lot going on right now. I know things can feel overwhelming or confusing or both – so let’s break it down. First, some basics on the Constitution: The Constitution gives Congress the power of the purse – meaning it is Congress’s responsibility to decide how money gets spent. Congress is also who makes the laws. * The role of the executive branch is to implement the laws that Congress makes. So what is happening right now? First, Donald Trump tried to freeze all federal funding (meaning all Congressionally appropriated spending that Congress has already voted on how to spend). Second, Elon Musk has tried to dismantle USAID, the agency that was Congressionally created and primarily responsible for U.S. foreign assistance. And third, Elon Musk and his private team (which is apparently made up of six young men aged 19-24 who have never worked in government before) are trying to gain access to really sensitive and critical payment systems and people’s personal information. All together, these actions amount to an attempted coup. This is illegal. The executive branch has to follow the laws that Congress has passed – they can’t just do whatever they want. That’s not how our government works! So, obviously... this is bad. It’s very, very bad and very dangerous. And while Democrats don’t have as many tools available to us in the minority, we are taking action. Here’s our four-part strategy to counter this attempted coup: 1. We’re introducing legislation to address some of these things , like stopping Elon Musk’s takeover of USAID or his team’s access to sensitive systems. This is the main action Members of Congress have the power to take, and we are absolutely doing it. 2. Lawsuits are already underway , and we’ve seen that they can be successful. Federal judges have already forced a pause on the federal funding freeze Trump tried to put in place, and blocked his executive order trying to end birthright citizenship. It’s important to note that Congress cannot itself file lawsuits – so we need to work with outside partners, like Democratic state attorneys general or impacted nonprofit organizations – to be able to use this tool. 3. Congress will do our constitutionally mandated oversight. I went with my colleagues this week to USAID HQ and the Treasury Department to see what’s happening with our own eyes, and we will keep demanding answers. 4. Lastly, we need you. Public pressure is the best tool that we have to actually push back on what Donald Trump is doing and get him to pause or reverse it. Speak out about how his actions are affecting you. Organize within your community. Bring this up with your friends. I know things feel chaotic, especially with our 24-hour news cycle with frenzied coverage of every comment Trump makes. But please don’t tune out – we need you and your voice now more than ever before. Thanks for reading, Sara [] Congresswoman Sara Jacobs California’s 51st District CONTRIBUTE [] [] Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress Sara Jacobs for Congress PO Box 120085 San Diego, CA 92112 United States www.facebook.com/SaraJacobsCA/ [www.facebook.com/SaraJacobsCA/] twitter.com/sarajacobsca [twitter.com/sarajacobsca] www.instagram.com/sarajacobsca/ [www.instagram.com/sarajacobsca/] CONTRIBUTE TODAY [] We believe these emails are a critical way for Sara to stay in touch with supporters and constituents. # I tried to raise money for the wokest USAID DEI programs, I didn't expect THIS to happen...
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/9833f34a-8082-4be7-8491-efc5df844126?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/9833f34a-8082-4be7-8491-efc5df844126?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
PolitiBrawl Editor-In-Chief Matt Miller tried his damndest to raise money for some of USAID's absurd woke DEI programs recently slashed by Elon Musk and President Donald Trump.
"Do you care about transgender musicals in Ireland? Any little bit helps!"
What happened next is unbelievable, watch it right here:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/9833f34a-8082-4be7-8491-efc5df844126?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **MAGA congresswoman WRECKS CNN host for trashing Trump's USAID slashing**
GOP Rep. Nicole Malliotakis **[took a wrecking ball to CNN Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/647fda5e-59b6-434f-98b5-dbcbde8b7986?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for complaining about President Trump's cuts to USAID and their absurdly expensive woke programs.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/647fda5e-59b6-434f-98b5-dbcbde8b7986?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/647fda5e-59b6-434f-98b5-dbcbde8b7986?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"We cannot get any accountability from this agency!" Malliotakis said of USAID. "This is a real bureaucratic mess! The money is being shoveled to countries all over the world. Countries that hate us!"
She continued to explain that USAID spent millions on DEI programs in countries where people are starving and have much bigger problems.
**["It's ridiculous! These are facts, this is actually where our money is going to!!"](https://substack.com/redirect/647fda5e-59b6-434f-98b5-dbcbde8b7986?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Malliotakis erupted. "Don't you think this pause is warranted?!"
CNN's Abbey Philips pushed back, stating that some of the cut programs are important and valuable bipartisan programs and that elected officials in congress, such as Malliotakis, should be the ones to defund government programs.
"That's exactly what we are doing," Malliotakis fired back. "Our intention when we appropriated this money is not being spent that way,.. $50 million for Condoms in Gaza!!"
Watch the heated clash right here:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/647fda5e-59b6-434f-98b5-dbcbde8b7986?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**[Presented By 4PAtriots](https://substack.com/redirect/94588151-77f3-429d-9556-1ec69157dea3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# The Truth About The Coming Food Crisis
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/94588151-77f3-429d-9556-1ec69157dea3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**This might be your only warning.** According to official reports, dangerous global events have reached America, putting the entire nation in harm's way.
Despite this imminent crisis, mainstream media remains silent. And our own government ignores dire warnings of industry experts like fourth-generation American farmers who warn:
"Food shortages are coming."
**As a result of this crisis, survival food is more important than ever.**
If you don't take action or if you stockpile the wrong foods, you could be setting your family up to go hungry in a time of crisis.
**Well, no need to worry anymore.**
Obviously, waiting for the government to give me a handout in a disaster just wasn't an option for me. And I was completely turned off by the crazy high cost of survival food sold by some companies.
That's why I ordered and got my very own stockpile of these best-selling **[Survival Food Kits](https://substack.com/redirect/94588151-77f3-429d-9556-1ec69157dea3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
When you secure your own food stockpile, you will have the peace of mind and security knowing that when a crisis hits... You will be able to calmly reassure your family that they're safe and they will have plenty to eat.

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Black History Month in Virginia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
As we celebrate Black History Month this February, I’m reflecting on the many prominent Black Virginians and Americans who’ve made a mark on our Commonwealth and moved America forward.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101791891/527077513/-725726589?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmQ5OTY5N2ItNDJlNi1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=iUcgf0rVlm3jbbUROHCctl4EKUJoZqnftuaZlj3vfLw=&emci=2199697b-42e6-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=2d99697b-42e6-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=8470899)
---
| Peter,

As we celebrate Black History Month this February, I’m reflecting on the many prominent Black Virginians and Americans who’ve made a mark on our Commonwealth and moved America forward. From trailblazing leaders to unsung heroes, the contributions of Black Virginians have shaped the course of our history and continue to inspire Virginians across generations.

We honor the legacy of figures like Dr. Dorothy Height, whose lifelong work in civil rights and social justice brought meaningful change to our society, and the late John Mercer Langston, one of the first Black Americans elected to public office in the United States. We also acknowledge the tireless efforts of leaders today, from educators to local elected officials, who continue to advocate for equality and progress. Despite all of our great progress, there is more to be done.

Throughout this month, we reflect on the profound impact of these individuals, whose work has not only enriched our Commonwealth but has also been integral to the broader movement for justice and opportunity in America.

As we recognize their contributions, we must also commit ourselves to the ongoing work of creating a more inclusive future for all. The fight for justice is far from over, but by standing together, we can build on the legacy of those who have come before us.

Let us celebrate Black History Month by honoring the past, recognizing the present, and working toward a brighter future for everyone.

Sincerely,

Abigail Spanberger
---

body:
Someone is finally speaking up!!!
| FINALLY, we have Democratic heroes like Jamie Raskin FIGHTING BACK!!! [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86708?t=6&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH) **We know what this means:** _Trump, his MAGA trifecta, and his billionaire buddies _WILL_ go after Rep. Raskin, again (!) — _and we need to be prepared.__

We’re sending Rep. Raskin a letter tomorrow at 9 AM sharp to show our support and stand with him in the face of MAGA attacks: [**Will you join the top 99 Democrats in your state and add your name? »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86708?t=7&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH) | [ADD MY NAME: STAND WITH REP. JAMIE RASKIN »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86708?t=8&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH)
---
TO: Rep. Jamie Raskin
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank You! Rep. Raskin,

**I couldn’t be more thrilled to have your voice in the House at this critical time in our history — you are one of the most heroic Representatives we have, and I’m so grateful.**

As a key part of the January 6th committee, you stood up to Donald Trump and his MAGA Republicans over and over again. _Now the GOP is furious at you,_but you continue to stand strong and lead our party forward.__

[**I can’t thank you enough for all of the work you’ve done to hold MAGA Republicans accountable – so I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to VPP to keep electing incredible Democrats just like you. As long as you keep fighting for Democracy, we’ll be beside you! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86708?t=9&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH)

Standing with you, **_X A grateful Democrat_** | [ADD MY NAME: STAND WITH REP. JAMIE RASKIN »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/86708?t=10&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH)

-The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=18820%2E878179%2E8n1jCH).**

name: Newsletter via Our Revolution
subject: Unified opposition to the Trump-Musk takeover
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

INSIDER NEWS FROM THE FRONTLINES OF THE POLITICAL REVOLUTION

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

HEADLINES: Fight Trump & The Oligarchs | Stop Musk | The Climate Fightback
Begins | We Beat the Elite: DNC Election Results! | Building Down-Ballot
Resilience | & MORE!

★ ★ ★

While Congressional Democrats dither in DC, Trump and the Oligarchs are
aggressively moving to consolidate control over our government by engaging
in unlawful and unConstitutional acts by:

* Purging career civil servants and busting unions;
* Firing independent Inspector Generals and Commissioners of the EEOC,
FDA;
* Embedding loyalists and top lieutenants of CEOs in top posts;
* Gagging experts at agencies across the government, including the CDC
and EPA;
* Breaking into sensitive databases and mining citizen private data;
* Weaponizing the DOJ and the FBI against opponents...

Trump and the Oligarchs are trying to break the guardrails of our fragile
democracy. We will not let them.

We are organizing citizens across our Republic to stand up to this corrupt
regime, and we’re demanding a unified opposition from the Democratic
Congressional Leaders.

Collaboration is unacceptable.
That means no more Senate Dems breaking ranks to vote yes on any of
Trump’s dangerous nominees. It means no more House members shirking their
responsibility to hold the Oligarchs accountable. We must stand united to
stop the slide into authoritarian capitalism.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21442?t=1011&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]TELL YOUR SENATORS TO REJECT TRUMP NOMINEES
(We’ll deliver the message straight to their Senate offices, and keep up
the temperature with a flood of calls demanding the same).

★ ★ ★

★ ★ ★

We’re fighting for our future by mobilizing a Citizen Army of grassroots
activists to fight back against Trump and the Oligarchs, seizing the reins
of the Democratic Party, and electing the next generation of Progressives
in hundreds of down-ballot races across the country.

Read more below about how we’re fighting on our 3-point plan to Defeat
Trump & the Oligarchs, Transform the Democratic Party, and Elect
Progressives up and down the ballot in 2025.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21444?t=1012&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]It’s up to us to create a well-organized and effective opposition to
the Trump regime. We are not powerless in this moment, but we need your
help! Please pitch in a donation to help fund our fightback.

★ ★ ★

Oligarchy is no longer at the gate, it’s busting its way through the house
— and we need our movement to be activated.

This means getting in the streets, showing up in Senate offices, raising
the pressure through media and targeted actions, and mounting a public
show of force that cannot be ignored.

That’s why we’re building out our Citizen Action Team — 30,000 strong and
growing — with organizing networks in Washington D.C. and every
congressional district in the country.

Our Citizen Action Team will meet this Thursday, Feb. 13, at 8:30 pm ET /
5:30 PT. Hop on the Zoom to learn about the actions we’re already taking
and the different ways you can be involved as we Fight Trump and the
Oligarchs.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21445?t=1013&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]JOIN THE CALL!

★ ★ ★

★ ★ ★

Trump is on a mission to ‘Drill Baby Drill’ and he has forged an unholy
alliance between Big Oil and Big Tech to ravage our planet — but we are
not powerless.

This week, we’re kicking off our national End Polluter Handouts Campaign
to halt the worst of the Oil Oligarchs’ agenda.

Our powerful coalition - made-up of Our Revolution, Sunrise, Friends of
the Earth, Zero Hour, Greenpeace, the Hip Hop Caucus, Oil Change
International and dozens of other organizations - are joining forces to
block Trump’s money pipeline to Big Oil.

Sign-up to be part of our LIVE Climate Fightback Kickoff Call this Monday!
We’ll hear from leading climate organizers, including Aru Shiney-Ajay,
Director the Sunrise Movement; Erich Pica, President of Friends of the
Earth; Jaime Minden, Organizing Director of Zero Hour; and Tefere Gebre,
Chief Program Officer of Greenpeace USA.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21446?t=1014&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]RSVP NOW and we’ll send you a reminder to tune in LIVE at 8:30pm
ET/5:30 PT on Monday, Feb. 10th.

★ ★ ★

On Wednesday at 1pm ET at the US Capitol, our Coalition to End Polluter
Handouts will join Members of Congress to voice our public opposition to
Trump’s fossil-fueled frenzy. We’ll be joined by US Rep. Ilhan Omar and
other progressive champions.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21447?t=1015&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]Join us IN-PERSON on Capitol Hill!

This will be followed by a “Climate Revival” featuring award winning
actress / singer Antonique Smith and Rev. Lennox Yearwood of the Hip Hop
Caucus.

The event starts at 7pm at the Lutheran Church of the Reformation at 212
E. Capitol St. NE, Washington DC, 20003 — hope you can make it!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21444?t=1016&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]If you can, chip in a donation of any amount to help fund our climate
fightback against Trump and the Oil Oligarchs. Thank you!

★ ★ ★

★ ★ ★

BEATING BACK THE PARTY ELITE

To defeat Trump and the Oligarchs, we need a true Opposition Party that
isn’t bought by the billionaires. That’s why Our Revolution organized to
beat back the Political Establishment - and its billionaire backers - to
elect Ken Martin to serve as the new chair of the Democratic National
Committee.

Ken - chair of the Minnesota state party - defeated his main rival who was
backed by Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer, and bankrolled by billionaires
like Reid Hoffman and ‘liberal’ dark money groups.

Even though only 448 DNC party insiders got to vote, Our Revolution’s
grassroots-powered Citizen Action Team: Hold the Party Accountable worked
to make sure our voices were heard - we hosted the only grassroots DNC
Candidate Forum of the race, sent letters, and made calls.

Even though we’ve won, our work is not done. Our Revolution Leader Joseph
Geevarghese wrote in [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21434?t=1017&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]The Nation about how the grassroots must hold the
new Democratic leadership accountable.

“The decades-long dependence on billionaires has fundamentally undermined
the Democratic Party’s credibility as an opposition party. Democrats
claimed to be the party of the working class—while still taking money from
the same elites who rig the system,” Joseph wrote.

“If the party is serious about becoming a true opposition force, it must
now fully commit to a grassroots fundraising model—one that rejects
corporate influence and restores faith in its ability to fight for
working-class Americans.”

★ ★ ★

In addition to electing a new DNC chair, we also organized to elect Jane
Kleeb — an Our Revolution founding board member and climate activist — as
the next President of the Association of State Democratic Chairs (ASDC)
and Vice Chair of the Party.

Jane, who also serves as Chair of the Nebraska Democratic Party, is
committed to investing resources in State Parties and strengthening
grassroots organizing.

With true insurgents like Ken and Jane at the top of the Party, it’s time
to ramp up our work to hold Democrats accountable to working people, not
billionaires and corporate consultants.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21448?t=1018&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]Our Revolution is one of the only grassroots political groups doing
this work inside the party. Can you chip in to help us keep up the
pressure for change?

★ ★ ★

With our impact on the direction of new DNC leadership, we now have a
foothold to push for real change in the party. So far, more than 10,000
grassroots volunteers, donors and local leaders have signed on demanding
these necessary party reforms.

It’s time for the Democratic party to reject corporate money, increase
transparency and accountability and rebuild a multiracial, working-class
base. Let’s get to work to grow our power in the party, recenter the
working class, regain voter trust, and win crucial elections.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21147?t=1019&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]SIGN THE PETITION

★ ★ ★

2025 isn’t an off-year for elections -- in fact, there are thousands of
races at the state and local level with progressives running for mayor,
city council, state offices, and more.

Our goal is to build power. Our movement elected a progressive voting bloc
in Congress with the Progressive Caucus — and this year is a chance to do
the same in cities and counties across the country.

Proof of concept: In St. Louis, Our Revolution Board Member Megan Green
ran for city alderman and realized she needed allies serving alongside her
to move a progressive agenda. So she partnered with Our Revolution and ran
progressive slates over multiple cycles, creating a governing majority in
the city — from mayor to council and more.

If progressives are going to build power in the United States, we have to
return to our roots and do it from the bottom up.

If you’re a candidate running for election or re-election, join Megan
Green and the Our Revolution Political Team for a 2025 Candidate Meetup to
learn about our endorsement process and how we support candidates with
strategic resources to win elections and build power for the long haul.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21149?t=1020&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]REGISTER HERE (ANNOUNCED CANDIDATES ONLY)

★ ★ ★

The Citizen Action Team meets this Thursday, Feb. 13, at 8:30 pm ET / 5:30
PT. Hop on the Zoom to learn about the actions we’re already taking and
the different ways you can be involved as we Fight Trump and the
Oligarchs.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21445?t=1021&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]JOIN OUR CITIZEN ACTION TEAM CALL

★ ★ ★

Trump is on a mission to ‘Drill Baby Drill’ and he has forged an unholy
alliance between Big Oil and Big Tech to ravage our planet — but we are
not powerless.

Sign-up to be part of our LIVE Climate Fightback Kickoff Call this Monday
at 8:30p ET / 5:30 PT! We’ll be strategizing with leading climate
organizers, including Aru Shiney-Ajay, Director the Sunrise Movement;
Erich Pica, President of Friends of the Earth; Jaime Minden, Organizing
Director of Zero Hour; and Tefere Gebre, Chief Program Officer of
Greenpeace USA.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/21446?t=1022&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]RSVP HERE

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/17429?t=1023&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]WHEN WE ORGANIZE, WE WIN!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13120?c0=revreport.T4WXik&t=1008&akid=7677%2E5885482%2E2lyoC8 ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the

Friend, Every day and every hour, our communities are under attack by a lawless president and his administration, which is led by his cabinet of billionaire bullies. **Congresswoman Rashida Tlaib has recorded a message for you, reminding you that dissent is patriotic and that she will never stop fighting for you:**
> _“As your Congresswoman, every moment that they attack or come after any of you, I will be right there blocking them, even if I have to put my body on the line.”_
[**Click here to watch Rashida’s message and share it with family and friends on social media.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.RNKlYhK-gnABEpCnH88uwF4aoIEw3v6XnCWN8HvY1xIrmBVkdHPXuJcJ7ZYd5Q4UpsUfX3mH0VZOGxp5LBeeZWFKiukdxceH31AMi9pRyEs2K2kKWUExHOJFDH960_YjQptPWHK95Pc_mTvOmMbKD_jJOC3-qaXNAt_VJX_8vQS2BUC5zb-86moQA3PWS6Kek2pO5Pfx9MEJgC04QhbI2IhYqyNPCyIbv64SY5M1rRuWYmjen0R3_lzKeWqF75RBrbnb8BJJYWfj66WuCXOx8j74oapPizipmh7d0vEoUT8Qj3uw_phcI3D2jzUakYR7BNYBCfLiNxG2VXRCqyoVMQ/4du/h6Zq3VdJSC-7ihmBo4i5Fg/h1/h001.NwfzScmuRlluYOnYmYIQB-dy9eH6FOTy8om7B_9F48E) [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.RNKlYhK-gnABEpCnH88uwF4aoIEw3v6XnCWN8HvY1xKToysFhUzKXo4x5yQtCALwQkaYPqmqPK7CAS_vvQWpgh-LOFB0rC-PaPAdZXtxFykP_ILCNMGxdIhB6PNNYCrshTctAeFKuPgmfOZR4UUKlJQdezoXp-ZjXO25QpRMNmXPQS_EZX_8-iWwEmFxc8I6jzMz_QM_vsAXZ-18y3bRc1l6wXiVF8cvsnur2z2ywj5h4VwqRbmM3I_Yfgu0OF3_UqAF9qGWEzsgUiEwQjTLdXTP4M6nVdczODwkNsKrDmyMWipXF5AeAPxGtr5jDmPbwAzOXpPxlyI9g4zOIB7bCw/4du/h6Zq3VdJSC-7ihmBo4i5Fg/h2/h001.7dXXR7gTrQTdqk6aLGh6DPTC0kFoXCUHrDqzEIrblH4) | [SHARE ON FACEBOOK](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.RNKlYhK-gnABEpCnH88uwF4aoIEw3v6XnCWN8HvY1xJG__iKtIxwJaCzm2F3mGi1m1zzkza_68NllCcpVSiT7foxLq0yr0WYJVO2LKtQcWo_WLHXAPJEOyh1uO51JUkEz3iy51KgFEEasu04AiNx59bGpwt1ND7sXKcMT6u2xhoieS3ehvhIqdVrqcWjgF0_-e6r6olJOY-Ywst2r7zf_8WS0tyLWSikKk5Mj8DGY0IDKYEmk4UmbCn9DMq9PszxNqnYnD8MG8H14YzhejqTldxZ_H2M6iRs7NKetL9NXAG2aTa6ke7V5OfhDbX-G_LNH9EV3LQidTJ8p_gNw9E9Jg/4du/h6Zq3VdJSC-7ihmBo4i5Fg/h3/h001.Xub2V2W9Ug9FgUYCaGXHd_66MDylu83hYaHVdIF29jw)

Friend,

We’re coming off the most important first week of any American
presidency in history. President Trump made a lot of promises on the
campaign trail, and it’s stunning to see how many he’s delivered
on in such a short time.

But I made some promises, too.

From the moment President Trump announced Kash Patel as his nominee to
restore integrity at the FBI, I vowed to lead the charge to get him
confirmed—and now we’ve met our moment.

I introduced Kash Patel at his confirmation, and he was immediately
met with baseless attacks and cheap shots from the Left.

Despite the Democrats’ desperate attempts to derail the hearing,
Kash held firm and demonstrated he is the leader we need at the FBI.

Now, I want to hear from you.

DO YOU THINK THE SENATE SHOULD CONFIRM KASH PATEL AS OUR NEXT FBI
DIRECTOR?
[https://secure.winred.com/nrsc/cabinet-confirmations-poll-promises-tt-e-wama/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=wama_nrsc&utm_campaign=20250206_na_cabinetconfirmationspollpromises-tt-v1_ttillis_nrsc&utm_content=fundraising&recurring=true&amtposition=4&utm_term=lrm-active]

ANSWER POLL
[https://secure.winred.com/nrsc/cabinet-confirmations-poll-promises-tt-e-wama/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=wama_nrsc&utm_campaign=20250206_na_cabinetconfirmationspollpromises-tt-v1_ttillis_nrsc&utm_content=fundraising&recurring=true&amtposition=4&utm_term=lrm-active]

I refuse to back down from the Dems' attacks on Trump’s America
First agenda, and I am fighting tooth and nail to confirm Kash as FBI
Director.

WILL YOU STAND WITH ME AS I WORK TO GIVE KASH PATEL AND PRESIDENT
TRUMP’S OTHER NOMINEES A FAIR CONFIRMATION PROCESS?
[https://secure.winred.com/nrsc/cabinet-confirmations-poll-promises-tt-e-wama/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=wama_nrsc&utm_campaign=20250206_na_cabinetconfirmationspollpromises-tt-v1_ttillis_nrsc&utm_content=fundraising&recurring=true&amtposition=4&utm_term=lrm-active]

Sincerely,
#
[https://secure.winred.com/nrsc/cabinet-confirmations-poll-promises-tt-e-wama/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=wama_nrsc&utm_campaign=20250206_na_cabinetconfirmationspollpromises-tt-v1_ttillis_nrsc&utm_content=fundraising&recurring=true&amtposition=4&utm_term=lrm-active]

name: Yvette Martinez
subject: I hope you are hanging in there.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Hi there. I hope you are hanging in there.
Despite all of our work and progress in the 2024 Election — even flipping three U.S. House seats from red to blue — Donald Trump is our President. He’s working overtime with Elon Musk and his cronies to rig the government against everyday Americans and for the billionaires.
It’s tough to watch.
What keeps me motivated through all this, however, is the strength and determination from our California Strong leaders . California is a tenacious state, and we won’t be easily bullied by Donald Trump or National Republicans.
For example, newly elected Congressman George Whitesides has hit the ground running giving the first House Democratic Weekly Address of the term and using it to fight for wildfire relief for Californians.
With Republicans threatening to condition aid on California adopting right-wing policies, Rep. Whitesides is standing up and speaking out. George Whitesides flipped a district from red to blue in 2024 — he literally would not be in this fight without you .
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount=]Or what about California Attorney General Rob Bonta ? AG Bonta knows you can’t change the United States Constitution with an executive order, but that is exactly what Donald Trump is trying to do — redefining citizenship with the stroke of a pen.
This is blatantly unconstitutional, which is why AG Bonta is taking Donald Trump to court.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount=]And who could forget California’s newly-elected Senator, Adam Schiff . Senator Schiff has made clear that if Donald Trump continues to let Elon Musk run wild — randomly shutting down whatever departments or offices he feels like — then he’ll personally hold up all of Donald Trump’s State Department nominees until order is restored.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount=]Corruption. Incompetence. Extortion. It’s heartbreaking to see from this new administration.
But what inspires me — and keeps me going in this work — are the bold California Strong leaders who are still standing and fighting.
Will you help the California Democratic Party empower our California Strong leaders by making a donation today? Your contribution will ensure we have the resources to both fight back against Donald Trump right now and plan for the 2026 election where we have the opportunity to take back the U.S. House. We flipped three seats from red to blue last year, narrowing the GOP to a historically small Majority. Now we need to finish the job and place a critical “check” on Donald Trump’s unhinged Presidency. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?refcode=&amount=]

CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb25?&refcode=&amount=]Thank you so much,
- Yvette
Executive Director
California Democratic Party

DONATE » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=&amount=20.24]
Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action.

## From pop culture, to MAGA-insider, my full conversation with Jessica Reed Kraus of House InHabit...
She explains how actually having a front row seat to Donald Trump and the movement changed her mind.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/20e7ece9-2493-495a-a8eb-82c8c9534deb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
## Getting It Done...
My full take on how the PACE of the Trump administration is insanely different from the Biden administration, is below the paywall
Become a VIP to read it below.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..r5bS3vrhLzNTCLlLw6pMHtrwdaEv_506Xum9xb9TG8g?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=156695325)

---|---|---
## Getting it done...
Can you believe its only been seventeen (and a half) days of the Trump White House 2.0?

Are you exhausted from trying to keep up? I am. I feel like when I walk away from the tv for a few minutes, I will miss an announcement of some sort. I was walking out of a meeting the other day when I remembered Karoline Leavitt would be holding a briefing at 1 pm. I pulled out my phone, went into my DirecTV app, and recorded it so I could watch it all later. ...

name: Robert Garcia
subject: Three Ways to Stop Elon Musk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress
body:
We must come together and fight.
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101794746/527085295/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZWFjMzlkMC00N2U2LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XK-X3PE8Y-B45RKe0CM3cceUUJpC3rdQO-BaFXtFOW0=)
Team,
Elon Musk is trying to dismantle our country brick by brick - but I’m here to stop him with your help.
I was just at the Department of Labor where the DOGE cronies were tying to seriously damage the department, but instead, _we shut down their meeting_.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101794747/527085296/1521319987?refcode=em250208&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZWFjMzlkMC00N2U2LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XK-X3PE8Y-B45RKe0CM3cceUUJpC3rdQO-BaFXtFOW0=)
Next week, we’re facing our first hearing for the DOGE committee, where Marjorie Taylor Greene will be leading the Republicans to defend the illegal actions that Elon and Trump are conducting. **But Elon is coming after me.**
[So I am asking, Friend, can you contribute to my campaign today so we can be ready to fight back? Your support will help us push back even harder and ensure we’re ready for whatever comes next.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/101794748/527085297/1521319987?refcode2=3eac39d0-47e6-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&refcode=em250208&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZWFjMzlkMC00N2U2LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XK-X3PE8Y-B45RKe0CM3cceUUJpC3rdQO-BaFXtFOW0=)

name: Scalise, Steve
subject: Here’s where things get ugly:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
New York Democrats are floating a scheme to DELAY the Special Election. Why would they do that, you ask?
And it could endanger President Trump’s entire legislative agenda!
| |
---
| | | | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=64b3ad78bd0d4bdd83216b1b9dbeea8c&_e=NP6eYFW9H3XewBcGFn_Pm5oOJvrqNWK2TgApoRItrKZ0T1Plwko5NjCZQfOaGlCLK960Q2o3Ou_L3BjNdg3HxqGf3vNmf8Jo6jdZA-8UL-32C_KXTTMeFuLKJqN7MH15h6q7EeCP5PQFu61gBMVBWZgYip6NOCZuQRLHhOD_4cemRvTCghlIvwuhLr5be-VueX53i0DsFBw5A2mqErrcfkXwiRJafEu-pJpp1lzjwRBw47Blf5xNRI6yTZ9k6F2GUUYB4VFv8Zc0d4Zn0diUnN5LBP2SC5fVPO3MdNGe39c-0H-zGKC_74u3bOCJl8Lp_lVOYrnrLeTaM9FFbpla-t_1dGgRBo7FtiW9F8QoPZvaT1gx7t6VZsGL6dygsF5m5bvwa1Vrikvdpn2jaCHerA%3D%3D "Majority Leader Steve Scalise")
---

VERY few people are paying attention to this...

**And it could endanger President Trump 's entire legislative agenda!**

By all accounts, Elise Stefanik's confirmation to Trump's Cabinet will sail through. She's an excellent pick - no surprise there. That tees up a Special Election in New York to replace her. Then, the Governor is mandated by state law to lay out the election calendar.

Here's where things get ugly.

**New York Democrats are floating a scheme to DELAY the Special Election. Why would they do that, you ask? Well... it might be because it's a Conservative-leaning district, and Republicans have a _razor-thin_ margin in the House. **

_Losing one seat - or even postponing a Republican victory - will have serious ramifications for President Trump's agenda to pass through Congress. _

Democrats are so desperate to stop President Trump that they're willing to rewrite state law AND deny representation in Congress to hundreds of thousands of Americans.

What a disgrace.

_**[I won't forget this. Neither should you. That's why I'm launching an emergency fundraising blitz to fight back and save our House Majority.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=64b3ad78bd0d4bdd83216b1b9dbeea8c&_e=NP6eYFW9H3XewBcGFn_Pm5oOJvrqNWK2TgApoRItrKZ0T1Plwko5NjCZQfOaGlCLK960Q2o3Ou_L3BjNdg3HxqGf3vNmf8Jo6jdZA-8UL-32C_KXTTMeFuLKJqN7MH15h6q7EeCP5PQFu61gBMVBWZgYip6NOCZuQRLHhOD_4cemRvTCghlIvwuhLr5be-VueX53i0DsFBw5A2mqErrcfkXwiRJafEu-pJpp1lzjwRDsbSm9iVYOrG9gXtC_K7Tv4MZ_famIMqAIiWsAGWIN2qtOkq9StXuQWpjt8q7gtLUGczdReX-QybXcYPhG7RUPiSNyHultaEm4VP0uNi6vc2yiBUSdwXO55_9s9lhMJ0AVFtoTdXoZUMyw7ZCnFpfNlmff4lRvQ9niBv2hEYdIMA%3D%3D "I won’t forget this. Neither should you. That’s why I’m launching an emergency fundraising blitz to fight back and save our House Majority.")**_ I set a $100,000 goal and I'd really appreciate it if you'd be willing to pitch in a few bucks today.
| | [CONTRIBUTE NOW](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=64b3ad78bd0d4bdd83216b1b9dbeea8c&_e=NP6eYFW9H3XewBcGFn_Pm5oOJvrqNWK2TgApoRItrKZ0T1Plwko5NjCZQfOaGlCLK960Q2o3Ou_L3BjNdg3HxqGf3vNmf8Jo6jdZA-8UL-32C_KXTTMeFuLKJqN7MH15h6q7EeCP5PQFu61gBMVBWZgYip6NOCZuQRLHhOD_4cemRvTCghlIvwuhLr5be-VueX53i0DsFBw5A2mqErrcfkXwiRJafEu-pJpp1lzjwRBc5enEG-RJVzmkRZEIxteKW5w2RxZLKpzUUejomWOIuj9eVyH4YqElZJPvaX_cySVMbZHbCQnn7OBySqTZUwEPVGCW0daFlpX_NW04nmZTNCuGmSkX02UvG_ZEN4dOnQ2qmEsfY-Z-AWvz-c10NU7gebr5r6A6jBS3QI8IWIZB_Q%3D%3D "CONTRIBUTE NOW")

Let me be very clear:

Passing President Trump's agenda is at the very top of my priority list. We need to get this right and time is of the essence.

Every seat matters a LOT when the margins are so close.

I won't stand around while a bunch of New York Democrats are trying to hamstring President Trump's entire legislative agenda.

_**[Can you please help us? I really need your support right now. It doesn't get more important than this.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=64b3ad78bd0d4bdd83216b1b9dbeea8c&_e=NP6eYFW9H3XewBcGFn_Pm5oOJvrqNWK2TgApoRItrKZ0T1Plwko5NjCZQfOaGlCLK960Q2o3Ou_L3BjNdg3HxqGf3vNmf8Jo6jdZA-8UL-32C_KXTTMeFuLKJqN7MH15h6q7EeCP5PQFu61gBMVBWZgYip6NOCZuQRLHhOD_4cemRvTCghlIvwuhLr5be-VueX53i0DsFBw5A2mqErrcfkXwiRJafEu-pJpp1lzjwRBHEDt74jJmqIUEVWkvkgn16ERZkW6ktVW_wKLY-mU-7ct5QR1nGuZpOMi3AedF3GgRupRyvBcnkrQUfgdyQ90Y6I_YayOnZmmujt2vzBJr7iUf8StBPnRKjD1hwPd8v858e96nwaaWVcEYioMsx5DARiDxM_Pvtw6GDBRrI5FVUw%3D%3D "Can you please help us? Thanks for all you do,
Leader Scalise
